title: Golden-Glazed Smoky Uruguayan Pork Gratin
description: A date-night Uruguayan dish featuring smoky flavors, crisp texture and a hint of sweetness from applesauce. Paired well with a glass of red wine.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the shio-koji cure, brown sugar, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to make the cure.
Rub the cure all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Let the pork sit in the cure for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to penetrate.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the caramelized onions and cook for 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the applesauce and stir to combine.
Transfer the pork to a copper-mug baking dish and spoon the applesauce mixture on top of the pork.
Drizzle the apple cider vinegar over the top of the pork and sprinkle with fresh thyme.
Cover the mug with aluminum foil and bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
